card.intuition: An organized approach to addressing and managing the aftermath of a security breach or system outage, aiming to limit damage and reduce recovery time.
card.analogy: A hospital ER: when a 'Code Blue' (An Outage) is called, an ER team doesn't panic. They follow a pre-planned checklist: Stop the bleeding (Mitigate), find the cause (Diagnose), and perform surgery (Repair). After it's over, they review what happened (Post-mortem).
when_to_use: Every production outage or security alert! No matter how small
Coordinating multiple teams (SRE, Security, Dev) during a crisis
Ensuring that the 'Lessons Learned' are documented so the same bug never happens twice
gotchas: Blame-ful Post-mortems: if you focus on 'Who messed up', people will hide their mistakes. Focus on 'Why did the system allow this mistake to happen?'.
Communication Gap: a technical fix is useless if you haven't told the customers that the service is down and you're working on it.

exercise.prompt: Identify the document written AFTER an incident to describe what happened and how to avoid it in the future.
